workflow: Set review_decision to accepted or rejected.
Accepted entries must complete the curation block before fixture build.
Reviewed files can be passed to build_manual_fixture_from_review_candidates.py.

structured_payload_candidate.title: System Selection Criteria
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Design engineer must consider temperature, humidity, air motion, air/water velocity, water quality, outdoor air quality, indoor air purity, air changes, acoustics, climate, mold prevention, capacities, redundancy, spatial requirements, environmental health and safety, security, first cost, ROI, energy consumption, operator labor, maintenance costs, serviceability, reliability, flexibility, controllability, replaceability, life-cycle analysis, sustainability, seismic protection, filtration, and changing codes.

candidate_entries.judge_reason: Provides operational design criteria for system selection grounded in evidence

structured_payload_candidate.title: System Constraints
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Equipment and system constraints include performance limitations, code updates, available capacity, available space and access, available utility source, energy budget, equipment efficiency, operator knowledge, existing building occupants, and building architecture.

candidate_entries.judge_reason: Lists equipment and system constraints relevant to system design and operation, supported by evidence

structured_payload_candidate.title: Constructability Constraints
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Constructability issues include existing conditions, rigging equipment, demolition impact, maintaining occupancy, phasing installation, temporary HVAC, equipment availability, construction schedule, and construction budget.

candidate_entries.evidence_text:
This chapter addresses procedures for selecting
an appropriate system for a give n application while taking into
account pertinent issues associat ed with designing, building, com-
missioning, operating, and main taining the system. It also
addresses specific owner require ments and constraints associated
with selecting the optimum HVAC  system for the application.
Chapters 2  to 18 describe specif ic approaches and systems along
with their attributes, based on their heating and cooling medium,
the commonly used va riations, constructa bility, commissioning,
operation, and maintenance.
This chapter is intended as a guide for the design engineer,
builder, facility manage r, and student needing to know or reference
the analysis and selection process that ultimately leads to recom-mending the optimum system for the job.
candidate_entries.judge_reason: Addresses constructability constraints useful for planning and installation, supported by evidence

structured_payload_candidate.title: Decentralized System Characteristics
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Decentralized systems distribute cooling throughout the building, generally with direct-expansion cooling, electric or gas-fired heating, and occasionally oil-fired warm-air heating units.

candidate_entries.judge_reason: Describes characteristics of decentralized systems, useful for system selection guidance

structured_payload_candidate.title: Centralized System Characteristics
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Centralized systems are characterized by central refrigeration equipment with chilled-water distribution and central boiler equipment with hot-water distribution, including low-, medium-, and high-pressure steam plants and cogeneration systems.

candidate_entries.judge_reason: Describes centralized system characteristics, useful for system selection guidance

structured_payload_candidate.title: System Selection Report Contents
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The system analysis and selection report should include introduction, building program primary HVAC goals, criteria for selection, important factors, system integration, other HVAC goals, security and environmental health and safety criteria, building envelope, project timeline, basis of design, HVAC system analysis and selection matrix, system narratives, budget costs, and final recommendation(s).

candidate_entries.judge_reason: Outlines required contents of a system selection report, relevant to design process

structured_payload_candidate.title: System Selection Matrix Scoring
structured_payload_candidate.summary: A numerical rating system such as 0 to 10, with 10 equal to excellent and 0 equal to poor or not applicable, can provide a quantitative result for system selection.

candidate_entries.evidence_text:
In addition,
HVAC systems deemed inappropriate should be noted as having
been considered but not found appl icable to meet the owner’s pri-
mary HVAC goal.
The report should include an HVAC system selection matrix
that identifies the one or two suggested HVAC sy stem selections
(primary and secondary, when applic able), system c onstraints, and
other constraints and considerations. In completing this matrix
assessment, the design engineer should identify the owner’s input
to the analysis. This input can also  be applied as weighted multipli-
ers, because not all criteria ca rry the same weighted value.
Many grading methods are availabl e to complete an analytical
matrix analysis. Probably the simplest is to rate each item excel-
lent, very good, good, fair, or poor . A numerical rating system suchas 0 to 10, with 10 equal to excellent and 0 equal to poor or not
applicable, can provide a quantitative result.
candidate_entries.judge_reason: Suggests a scoring method for system selection matrix, supported by specific evidence

structured_payload_candidate.title: Decentralized System Capacity Requirements
structured_payload_candidate.summary: A decentralized system usually requires each piece of equipment to be sized for zone peak capacity, unless the systems are variable-volume, and does not benefit as much from equipment sizing diversity as centralized systems.

candidate_entries.judge_reason: Explains capacity sizing requirements for decentralized systems, operationally relevant

structured_payload_candidate.title: Centralized System Capacity Diversity
structured_payload_candidate.summary: A central system usually allows the design engineer to consider HVAC diversity factors that significantly reduce installed equipment capacity, offering first-cost and operating-cost benefits.

candidate_entries.judge_reason: Discusses diversity factor benefits for centralized systems, operationally relevant

structured_payload_candidate.title: Decentralized System First Cost Advantage
structured_payload_candidate.summary: A decentralized system probably has the best first-cost benefit, enhanced by phasing in the purchase of equipment as needed.

candidate_entries.judge_reason: Highlights first-cost advantage of decentralized systems, relevant to application guidance

structured_payload_candidate.title: Centralized System Operating Cost Advantage
structured_payload_candidate.summary: A central system usually has the advantage of larger, more energy-efficient primary equipment compared to decentralized system equipment, and allows staging of equipment operation to match building loads while maximizing operational efficiency.

candidate_entries.judge_reason: Describes operating cost and efficiency advantages of centralized systems

structured_payload_candidate.title: Decentralized System Maintenance Cost
structured_payload_candidate.summary: A decentralized system can save maintenance cost when equipment is conveniently located and equipment size and associated components are standardized, but outdoor maintenance may be difficult during bad weather.

candidate_entries.judge_reason: Discusses maintenance cost considerations for decentralized systems

structured_payload_candidate.title: Centralized System Maintenance Cost
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The equipment room for a central system provides the benefit of being able to maintain HVAC equipment away from occupants in an appropriate service work environment, and there are usually fewer pieces of equipment to service.

candidate_entries.judge_reason: Discusses maintenance cost benefits of centralized equipment rooms
